name: Ghaznavid Empire
note: Founded by Sebüktigin, a Turkish slave-soldier (ghulam) who rose to govern Ghazni (modern Afghanistan) under the Samanids. His son Mahmud of Ghazni (998–1030) transformed it into a great empire, launching 17 raids into the Indian subcontinent — breaking the Hindu Shahi kingdoms and establishing Islam in the Punjab. The Ghaznavids were patrons of Persian language and literature (the Shahnameh was written for Mahmud). Destroyed by the Ghurids.

figures.name: Mahmud of Ghazni
figures.significance: Greatest Ghaznavid ruler; 17 raids into India; destroyed the Hindu Shahi kingdom; sacked the temple of Somnath (1025); patron of Persian literature — Ferdowsi's Shahnameh was dedicated to him

figures.name: Ferdowsi
figures.significance: Persian poet who wrote the Shahnameh (Book of Kings) at Mahmud's court — the national epic of Iran; preserved pre-Islamic Persian history and myth; one of the greatest works of world literature
